title: Recursion, Recurrences, and Divide-and-Conquer · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Break a large document and spatial search problem into independently checkable subproblems. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a recursive solver with recurrence evidence with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.
Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.
Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
